pattern: A substring of even length is an echo string when its first half equals its second half; use rolling hashes to check all O(n^2) candidate substrings in O(n^2) total time while deduplicating with a set.
hints: An echo substring has even length 2L and its first L characters are identical to its last L characters.
Fix the half-length L and slide two windows of size L across the string — whenever their hashes match, you have a candidate echo starting at position i.
Use a set of (start, half_len) or hash values to avoid counting the same echo substring twice.

edge_inputs.description: all same characters — many echo substrings
overlapping echo substrings, count only distinct ones
short echo pair at the end
